skill.name: runcomfy-cli
skill.description: Run any model on RunComfy from the command line. The runcomfy CLI provides one binary, one auth, and hundreds of model endpoints for image generation, video generation, editing, and more.
skill.summary: Generate images and videos using the RunComfy CLI from your terminal.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 174 static findings are false positives from parsing a markdown documentation file. External command detections are bash code blocks showing CLI usage examples. Network detections are documentation links to runcomfy.com. Filesystem detections are references to the CLI token storage path. The skill declares a narrow tool scope of Bash(runcomfy *) for a legitimate media generation CLI.

low_findings.title: False positive: markdown code blocks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: All 123 external_commands detections are bash code blocks and inline code formatting in documentation. The skill only executes runcomfy commands via its declared Bash(runcomfy *) tool scope. No arbitrary shell execution is performed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every external command detection maps to markdown code block syntax (```bash...```) or inline backtick formatting in a documentation file, not executable code.

low_findings.title: False positive: documentation URLs flagged as hardcoded network endpoints
low_findings.description: All 40 network detections are markdown links to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and skills.sh in documentation. The CLI only connects to model-api.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.net / *.runcomfy.com for downloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are legitimate documentation links and service endpoints for the RunComfy platform, not unauthorized external connections.

low_findings.title: False positive: token path references flagged as filesystem access
low_findings.description: The filesystem detections are documentation references to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json, explaining where the CLI stores authentication tokens. The skill does not directly access this file; the runcomfy CLI binary handles token storage internally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: References to the token path are explanatory text in documentation, not filesystem access commands performed by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: False positive: CLI subcommand and table formatting triggered blocker alerts
low_findings.description: The 'whoami' subcommand is a legitimate part of the runcomfy CLI for verifying authentication identity, not system reconnaissance. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are artifacts from markdown table formatting (pipe characters) in the exit codes table.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The 'whoami' string is a standard CLI subcommand name for identity verification. The 'weak crypto' detection aligns with markdown table rows containing pipe characters, not actual cryptography usage.

content.user_title: Generate AI media with the RunComfy CLI
content.value_statement: Creating AI-generated images and videos usually requires navigating complex APIs and writing boilerplate code. The RunComfy CLI skill lets you generate, edit, and transform media by running simple commands from your terminal, with automatic polling and download handling.
actual_capabilities: Install and authenticate the RunComfy CLI with device-code or token-based login
Run any model endpoint with JSON input and automatic polling until completion
Download generated output files to a specified directory with a 2 GiB size limit
Check job status and cancel running requests with Ctrl-C
Stream output in JSON format for piping into other command-line tools
Batch process multiple prompts from a file with shell loops
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI to be installed separately via npm or npx
Needs a valid RunComfy API token for authenticated model access
Output download is limited to files under 2 GiB per file
Does not provide real-time streaming progress for generation steps

use_cases.title: Generate marketing visuals
use_cases.description: Create product images, social media graphics, and promotional videos by describing what you want in natural language.

use_cases.title: Prototype creative concepts
use_cases.description: Rapidly iterate on artistic ideas by generating variations with different models, prompts, and parameters.

use_cases.title: Automate media pipelines
use_cases.description: Build scripts that batch-generate images or videos, process them, and integrate results into CI/CD or data workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a single image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image with RunComfy using the prompt 'a serene mountain landscape at golden hour, photorealistic, 8k'. Use the openai/gpt-image-2/text-to-image model and save it to ./output.

prompt_templates.title: Batch process from a file
prompt_templates.prompt: Read prompts.txt line by line and generate one image per prompt using the flux-2-klein model. Save all outputs to ./batch-output/ with a timestamp subfolder for each run.

prompt_templates.title: Check async job status
prompt_templates.prompt: Submit a video generation job with --no-wait to get a request_id, then check its status every 30 seconds until it completes. Download the final video when done.

prompt_templates.title: Pipe JSON output to processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image with --output json and --no-download, then extract the first image URL with jq and download it with curl into a specific folder.

output_examples.input: Generate a photorealistic image of a cat using GPT Image 2
output_examples.output: The agent runs: runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/text-to-image --input '{"prompt": "a photorealistic cat sitting on a windowsill"}'. It polls for status every 2 seconds, then downloads result.png to the current directory.

output_examples.input: Batch generate 5 images from my prompts file
output_examples.output: The agent creates a shell loop that reads prompts.txt, calls runcomfy for each line with a unique output directory, and reports the path of each downloaded file as it completes.

output_examples.input: Submit a video generation job without waiting
output_examples.output: The agent runs: runcomfy --output json run bytedance/seedance-v2/pro --input '{"prompt": "a robot walking through a forest"}' --no-wait. It extracts the request_id from JSON, stores it, and later checks status with runcomfy status <request_id>.

best_practices: Always use --output json when scripting or piping results into other tools
Store your RunComfy token in the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and container environments
Use --no-wait for long-running video generation jobs, then poll with runcomfy status later
anti_patterns: Do not pipe curl install scripts directly into a shell without reviewing them first
Do not pass shell variables directly into --input without proper JSON escaping
Do not ignore exit code 77; it means authentication failed and you need to log in again

faq.question: How do I install the RunComfy CLI?
faq.answer: Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli for a global install, or use npx -y @runcomfy/cli for one-shot execution without installation.

faq.question: Where is my API token stored?
faq.answer: The token is saved to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file permissions 0600. You can also set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable to override the file.

faq.question: Can I cancel a running job?
faq.answer: Yes. Press Ctrl-C while the CLI is polling. It sends a DELETE request to cancel the remote job before exiting.

faq.question: What happens if a generation fails?
faq.answer: The CLI returns a non-zero exit code. Code 69 means an upstream server error and is retryable. Code 65 means invalid input JSON. Code 77 means authentication failed.

faq.question: How do I download outputs to a specific folder?
faq.answer: Use the --output-dir flag followed by the target path. Use --no-download to skip downloading entirely and only return the result JSON.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without installing anything?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires the RunComfy CLI to be installed on your system. The skill will guide you through installation but cannot execute runcomfy commands without it.
